feed.title: Piscataquis County Probate Court
feed.subtitle: No probate-specific news/RSS feed exists at any of the 16 counties; monitored by full-page fingerprint of the official probate court page. Sitewide WordPress /feed/ exists but is not probate-specific. Mostly static jurisdiction/contact description.

entry.title: Page updated: Piscataquis County Probate Court
entry.content:
This page's content changed since the last check. Visit the page directly to review the update.
What changed (text excerpt): Removed: - Home - General Info - Employment Opportunities - Request For Bids - County News - General FAQs - Commissioners - Meeting Agendas - Meeting Minutes - Contact Commissioners Office - Finance - Budget Advisory Committee - Contact Finance - Maintenance - DA - Emergency Mgmt - About LEPC - Preparedness - Training - Forms - E-911 Officer - Contact EMA - Sheriff - In the Community - Investigations / Patrol - Dispatch / Communications - Civil Deputies - County Jail - Contact Sheriff’s Office - Probate - Deeds - Unorganized Territories - General Assistance - Vehicle Registry - Waste - Voting - Liquor License - Tax Valuation - Probate - The Probate Court is responsible for the settlement of estates. Estates are settled through the administration of wills and trusts and provide for the orderly disposition of assets to the appropriate recipients in a timely and effective manner. - The Probate Court is also responsible for the appointment of guardians and conservators, adoptions, and name changes for the citizens of Piscataquis County. Hearings on these matters are conducted before the Judge of Probate. - You may view all Piscataquis County probate records (with the exception of adoptions conducted after August 1953) at - www.maineprobate.net. - Document viewing is free. There is a charge to print copies of documents. Prices are listed on the website. - In exercising the jurisdiction of the [diff truncated]

entry.title: Page updated: Piscataquis County Probate Court
entry.content: This page's content changed since the last check. Visit the page directly to review the update.

What changed (text excerpt): Removed: - Home - General Info - Employment Opportunities - Request For Bids - County News - General FAQs - Commissioners - Meeting Agendas - Meeting Minutes - Contact Commissioners Office - Finance - Budget Advisory Committee - Contact Finance - Maintenance - DA - Emergency Mgmt - About LEPC - Preparedness - Training - Forms - E-911 Officer - Contact EMA - Sheriff - In the Community - Investigations / Patrol - Dispatch / Communications - Civil Deputies - County Jail - Contact Sheriff’s Office - Probate - Deeds - Unorganized Territories - General Assistance - Vehicle Registry - Waste - Voting - Liquor License - Tax Valuation - Probate - The Probate Court is responsible for the settlement of estates. Estates are settled through the administration of wills and trusts and provide for the orderly disposition of assets to the appropriate recipients in a timely and effective manner. - The Probate Court is also responsible for the appointment of guardians and conservators, adoptions, and name changes for the citizens of Piscataquis County. Hearings on these matters are conducted before the Judge of Probate. - You may view all Piscataquis County probate records (with the exception of adoptions conducted after August 1953) at - www.maineprobate.net. - Document viewing is free. There is a charge to print copies of documents. Prices are listed on the website. - In exercising the jurisdiction of the [diff truncated]
